title: Signal Intelligence Wiring & VADER Sentiment Upgrade
rationale: Phase 12 completed integration of TiltDetector, training loop, decision cadence, bridge health, cascade validation, and anomaly detection. Codebase gap analysis + web research reveals 5 critical integration gaps where existing features are computed but never influence readiness, plus one NLP upgrade opportunity. (1) BiasDetector scores extracted by ConversationProcessor but never applied as readiness penalty — cognitive biases are phantom. (2) ConversationProcessor.estimate_cognitive_load() exists (US-284) but ReadinessComputer uses stressor-count heuristic instead — real cognitive load analysis is dead code. (3) Decision cadence variability_score is computed (US-310) and stored in signal but the readiness penalty formula (0.8 + 0.2 * score) was already applied — however OverridePredictor predictions are never consumed by any downstream code. (4) Keyword-based sentiment extraction is Phase 1 heuristic — VADER compound scoring provides negation/emphasis/contrast-aware polarity with zero model loading. (5) ReadinessSignal has no bias_scores field — biases detected but lost before reaching Buddy. (6) No /predict CLI command exists despite OverridePredictor being fully trained.

stories.title: Wire BiasDetector scores as readiness penalty component
stories.description: ConversationProcessor.process_message() populates ConversationSignals.bias_scores via BiasDetector (US-293), but ReadinessComputer.compute() never reads these scores. Wire bias_scores through compute() as a post-component penalty: each detected bias above threshold (score > 0.5) reduces readiness by 3 points, capped at 15 points total penalty. Add bias_scores dict to ReadinessSignal for Buddy visibility.
acceptance: ReadinessComputer.compute() accepts optional bias_scores: Dict[str, float] parameter
Each bias with score > 0.5 applies a 3-point readiness penalty (max 5 biases = 15 points)
Bias penalty applied after V1/V2 scoring but before EMA smoothing
ReadinessSignal extended with bias_scores: Dict[str, float] field (default empty dict)
ReadinessSignal.to_dict() includes bias_scores in output
When no bias_scores provided, zero penalty applied (backward compatible)

At least 8 tests: no biases no penalty, single bias above threshold, single bias below threshold, multiple biases capped at 15, bias_scores in signal dict, backward compat with no param, all 4 bias types, bias + tilt combined penalty

stories.title: VADER compound sentiment scoring to augment keyword extraction
stories.description: Replace the keyword-only sentiment calculation in ConversationProcessor with VADER compound scoring. VADER handles negation ('not good' → negative), emphasis ('GREAT!!!' → strong positive), and contrast ('good but worried' → mixed) automatically. Keep existing keyword extraction for stressor/topic detection but use VADER compound score as the primary sentiment signal. This is the Phase 1→Phase 1.5 NLP upgrade mentioned in the module docstring.
acceptance: ConversationProcessor.__init__() creates VADER SentimentIntensityAnalyzer instance
New _compute_vader_sentiment(message: str) → float method returning -1 to +1 compound score
process_message() uses VADER compound to compute sentiment_score: (compound + 1) / 2 → 0-1 normalized
Existing keyword extraction preserved for stressor/topic detection (not replaced)
VADER score blended with keyword sentiment: 0.6 * vader + 0.4 * keyword (keyword as safety net)
Graceful fallback: if nltk/vader unavailable, log warning and use keyword-only (current behavior)

At least 8 tests: positive message high sentiment, negative message low, negation handled, emphasis handled, contrast/mixed signals, fallback when vader unavailable, blend ratio correct, neutral message ~0.5

stories.title: Wire cognitive load estimation into readiness computation
stories.description: ConversationProcessor.estimate_cognitive_load() (US-284) returns 0.0-1.0 from message structural complexity (sentences, questions, conditionals, topic shifts). Currently ReadinessComputer uses a stressor-count heuristic for cognitive_load_score. Wire the real estimate through: pass current message to estimate_cognitive_load(), blend with stressor-count score (0.5 * text_load + 0.5 * stressor_load), update cognitive_load_score in components. This activates the dead US-284 code.
acceptance: ReadinessComputer.compute() accepts optional message_text: str parameter for cognitive load estimation
When message_text provided, calls ConversationProcessor.estimate_cognitive_load(message_text)
Blends text-based load (inverted: 1.0 - text_load) with stressor-count score: 0.5 * each
cognitive_load_score in ReadinessComponents reflects blended value
When message_text is None or empty, falls back to stressor-count-only (backward compatible)
ConversationProcessor instance created lazily in ReadinessComputer (import + instantiate on first use)
At least 7 tests: simple message high score, complex message lower score, no message fallback, blend ratio, stressor+complex worst case, stressor+simple medium case, empty string fallback

stories.title: Wire OverridePredictor into ReadinessSignal + /predict command
stories.description: OverridePredictor (US-254/255) is fully implemented with training, sigmoid prediction, and OOD detection, but predictions are never consumed. Wire it into ReadinessSignal as override_loss_risk (0-1 probability that next override will lose). Add /predict CLI command to AuraCompanion that shows current override risk assessment. When override_loss_risk > 0.7, ReadinessComputer applies a 10-point penalty.
acceptance: ReadinessSignal extended with override_loss_risk: float = 0.0 field
ReadinessSignal.to_dict() includes override_loss_risk
ReadinessComputer.compute() accepts optional override_predictor parameter
When predictor available and trained, calls predict() with current context to get loss probability
override_loss_risk > 0.7 triggers 10-point readiness penalty (applied after bias penalty, before EMA)
AuraCompanion handles /predict command: shows current override loss risk, risk level label, and recommendation
Graceful fallback: untrained predictor returns 0.0 risk (no penalty)

At least 8 tests: no predictor no penalty, untrained predictor no penalty, low risk no penalty, high risk penalty, signal field present, predict command output, OOD returns conservative estimate, combined with bias penalty

stories.title: Readiness trend decomposition via STL with alert generation
stories.description: Current anomaly detection (US-313 EWMA) catches sudden drops but misses gradual trend decline vs seasonal patterns. Add ReadinessTrendAnalyzer using statsmodels STL decomposition. Decomposes readiness history into trend + seasonal + residual. Generates alerts: TREND_DECLINE (slope < -0.5 with anomalies), SEASONAL_PATTERN (informational), ANOMALY_SPIKE (residual > 2σ). Feed trend_direction into ReadinessSignal. Requires minimum 14 samples. Graceful degradation if statsmodels unavailable.
acceptance: New ReadinessTrendAnalyzer class with add_sample(score, timestamp) and decompose() methods
decompose() returns dict with trend_direction ('improving'/'stable'/'declining'), trend_slope, anomaly_count, seasonal_amplitude
readiness_alert() returns list of typed alerts with severity (HIGH/MEDIUM/INFO)
Minimum 14 samples before decomposition activates (returns 'insufficient_data' otherwise)
ReadinessSignal extended with trend_direction: str = 'stable' field
ReadinessComputer creates analyzer in __init__, feeds samples in compute(), sets trend_direction on signal
Graceful fallback: if statsmodels unavailable, log warning and return 'stable' (no crash)

At least 8 tests: insufficient data returns stable, declining trend detected, stable trend detected, improving trend detected, anomaly spike alert, seasonal pattern info, graceful no-statsmodels fallback, signal field populated

stories.title: Phase 13 integration tests + cross-component validation
stories.description: Phase 12 gap analysis revealed no integration tests crossing ConversationProcessor → ReadinessComputer → Bridge Signal boundaries. Write integration tests that verify: (1) bias scores flow from process_message to readiness penalty, (2) VADER sentiment changes readiness, (3) cognitive load from text analysis affects score, (4) override predictor risk gates readiness, (5) trend analyzer produces alerts after 14+ samples. Each test exercises the full pipeline, not mocked components.
acceptance: At least 3 integration tests that create real ConversationProcessor + ReadinessComputer instances (no mocks on production code)
Test 1: High-bias messages → process_message → extract bias_scores → compute() with bias_scores → readiness penalized
Test 2: Negative VADER sentiment → lower emotional_state_score → lower readiness
Test 3: Complex message → high cognitive load → lower cognitive_load_score → lower readiness
Test 4: OverridePredictor with high loss risk → readiness penalty applied → signal has override_loss_risk
Test 5: 15+ readiness samples → trend analyzer detects direction → signal has trend_direction
All integration tests use NEUTRAL_CIRCADIAN to isolate signal effects from time-of-day
At least 7 tests total across integration scenarios
